title: Four questions for AI-ready biological data - Nature Machine Intelligence
gist: A Nature Machine Intelligence piece framing the drive to make biological data 'AI-ready' as an accelerating global push that still lacks a settled definition, posing several open questions about what AI-readiness actually requires.

themes.theme: AI-ready biological data as an accelerating but undefined push

insights.claim: The worldwide effort to make biological data 'AI-ready' is accelerating.
insights.speaker: Nature Machine Intelligence
insights.so_what: Signals rising demand and investment around data curation/infrastructure for AI in life sciences; watch tooling and standards plays that make biological datasets model-ready.
insights.quote: The push to make biological data ‘AI-ready’ is accelerating worldwide.
insights.locator: The push to make biological data

insights.claim: There is no settled definition of what 'AI-ready' means for biological data, so the concept is best approached as a set of unresolved questions rather than an established standard.
insights.speaker: Nature Machine Intelligence
insights.so_what: The absence of an agreed standard is an opportunity: whoever defines and operationalizes 'AI-ready' criteria (curation, provenance, FAIR-style standards) can shape the market.
insights.quote: But to understand what AI-ready means for biological data, there are several questions to answer.
insights.locator: But to understand what AI-ready means

top_entities: Frederick National Laboratory for Cancer Research
